title: Automated ML Model Prediction and Data Annotation System
description: Built a lightweight ML assistant that analyzes datasets, recommends suitable models, and speeds up computer-vision annotation workflows.
situation: During internship work on surveillance datasets, model selection and manual image annotation consumed significant R&D time and slowed experiments.
task: Design a semi-automated real-time pipeline that evaluates dataset characteristics, recommends model families, and accelerates preprocessing and annotation without relying on BigQuery ML for prediction tasks.
actions: Implemented dataset diagnostics for data types, missing values, descriptive statistics, and feature correlation.
Built a rule-based plus meta-learning recommender with MCP API signals to rank models by dataset profile and historical outcomes.
Added support for classification, regression, clustering, time-series, matrix factorization, and anomaly workflows.
Enabled TensorFlow batch prediction pipelines and hyperparameter tuning through Vertex AI Vizier.
Created an annotation assistant using YOLOv5, OpenCV, and LabelImg for pre-labeling with interactive correction UI.
currentWork: Enhancing edge-device feedback loops and annotation throughput on Raspberry Pi class devices for assistive scenarios.
result: Reduced model selection effort by about 60 percent and annotation effort by about 40 percent, improving experimentation speed and model fit quality.

title: Autonomous Workflow Optimization with Intelligent Agents
description: Developed a multi-agent workflow optimization platform for dynamic tasking, error handling, and real-time decision support.
situation: Manual enterprise workflows were rigid, intervention-heavy, and lacked real-time adaptive control.
task: Create an AI-first system that autonomously optimizes workflows, learns from historical context, and adapts to changing runtime inputs.
actions: Integrated reinforcement-learning and LLM-driven agents for task prioritization, resource balancing, and exception routing.
Designed role-specific multi-agent components for prioritization, monitoring, and dynamic orchestration.
Built real-time insight and monitoring dashboards to surface workflow health and optimization recommendations.
Implemented adaptive allocation logic based on workload pressure, priority, and agent performance.
Deployed serverless workflows with AWS Lambda and integrated enterprise systems via RPA connectors.
result: Reduced manual workflow effort by approximately 50 to 70 percent while improving operational responsiveness and decision quality.

title: Credit Card Usage Analysis and User Activity Prediction
description: Built user segmentation and activity prediction models with dashboard-based behavioral insights for personalization and fraud monitoring.
situation: Financial datasets often lacked practical behavior intelligence for personalization and early fraud detection.
task: Analyze spending behavior, cluster user cohorts, forecast activity, and identify anomalous transactions to support product and risk teams.
actions: Performed end-to-end data cleaning, profiling, and exploratory analysis on historical card usage.
Created customer segments through clustering and trained predictive models for user activity forecasting.
Applied classification, regression, sequence modeling, and anomaly detection techniques for behavior risk signals.
Developed Power BI dashboards for category-level, temporal, and frequency-based spending analysis.
result: Reached about 87 percent activity prediction accuracy and enabled stronger campaign targeting with earlier anomaly visibility.

title: Generative AI Solution for Large-Scale Domain-Specific NLP Tasks
description: Built a generative NLP solution tuned for domain context to improve sentiment and content-generation quality.
situation: Traditional NLP baselines struggled with nuanced language in domain-specific unstructured datasets.
task: Deliver a high-accuracy generative NLP workflow for sentiment and content generation on specialized corpora.
actions: Integrated GPT-4 through the OpenAI API and curated focused domain datasets for adaptation.
Fine-tuned and iteratively evaluated model behavior for higher contextual fidelity.
Optimized prompts and evaluation loops to better capture subtle linguistic variation in real user content.
result: Improved NLP output quality and delivered roughly 28 percent accuracy uplift for targeted business use cases.

title: Pizzeria - Full-Stack Web Application for Custom Pizza Ordering
description: Delivered a modular full-stack ordering platform with customization flows and service-oriented backend design.
situation: Conventional food-ordering flows lacked robust customization and responsive order lifecycle tracking.
task: Build a scalable web app for custom pizza ordering with clean frontend UX and modular backend services.
actions: Built dynamic React interfaces with hooks for interactive ordering and real-time state updates.
Implemented Node.js and Express APIs for order flow, inventory checks, and payment operations.
Used MongoDB for user and order persistence with a schema suitable for extensible options.
Split backend responsibilities into microservice-style modules to support future scale and feature growth.
result: Improved ordering usability and operational flow with an architecture ready for loyalty and delivery-tracking extensions.

title: Object Detection with Audio Feedback for Visually Impaired Users
organization: Sri Chandrasekharendra Saraswathi Vishwa Mahavidyalaya, Kancheepuram
description: Created an edge-deployable assistive vision system that detects and tracks objects, then provides real-time audio guidance.
situation: Visually impaired users often lack reliable, low-latency assistive detection for safe navigation in unfamiliar surroundings.
task: Build a lightweight object detection and tracking pipeline with audio feedback on resource-constrained hardware.
actions: Implemented YOLOv3 plus Deep SORT for robust object detection and multi-object tracking.
Optimized model execution and video processing flow for Raspberry Pi deployment constraints.
Integrated OpenCV processing and text-to-speech alerts triggered by object class and proximity.
result: Delivered reliable real-time assistive detection with low-latency edge inference and practical mobility support potential.

title: Speech Emotion Recognition Using Machine Learning
organization: Sri Chandrasekharendra Saraswathi Vishwa Mahavidyalaya, Kancheepuram
description: Developed an audio-based emotion classifier using engineered speech features and supervised learning.
situation: Emotion inference from speech is difficult due to speaker variability and signal-level acoustic variation.
task: Classify emotion categories from raw speech samples for improved human-computer interaction scenarios.
actions: Extracted MFCC, pitch, and short-term energy features from the RAVDESS dataset.
Preprocessed audio streams and engineered robust feature vectors for model training.
Trained and evaluated supervised classifiers to distinguish emotional states from voice patterns.
result: Built a strong baseline classifier for emotional speech recognition suitable for assistive and conversational AI integration.
